question: What has been written or said about Blend Capital across Stellar news, research, and talks?

golden.answer: Resolve **Blend Capital on Stellar** (not blend.com), then return a compact set of actual **dated, source-linked** news, research, talks/AV, proposals, or SCF coverage from the live content results. Give only enough identity context to disambiguate it as the Soroban lending protocol. Each item should retain its own title or topic, date where available, source, and URL; the set may change as the corpus changes. Do not make current project status, a fixed SCF amount, or a remembered partnership a requirement of this content-discovery answer.
keyFacts: Returns genuinely retrieved, dated, source-linked coverage about the Stellar Blend lending protocol across the available content types.
avoid: Do NOT return coverage about blend.com as if it were the Stellar protocol.
Do NOT fabricate articles, quotes, partnerships, or require volatile identity/funding facts that were not returned by the cited content records.
golden.notes:
GROUNDED 2026-06-22 (re-verified 2026-06-29): Blend confirmed as the Stellar Soroban lending/money-market protocol (Scout: Lending, Live) — 'a lending and borrowing protocol (money market) on Stellar Soroban... permissionless, isolated lending pools.' This is the CONTENT-DISCOVERY lane (resolve→call, multiple sourced news/research/talks/SCF items) → lumenloop_find_content_about_project — DISTINCT from q-defi-blend-what-is (lumenloop_get_project; single identity record). KEY trap is entity resolution: blend.com (US bank-lending company) is NOT this Blend.
Also good if the answer: Frames Blend as the Stellar lending money-market primitive when surfacing coverage.
Golden cites: Each surfaced item carries its source (Lumenloop content record).
Corrected 2026-07-10: this lane grades live content discovery and entity resolution. Status and SCF funding belong to their own cases and must not become hidden gates here.

corroboration.claim: The live content operation returns dated, sourced Blend coverage across multiple content types
